name: Random Background (d46)
description: Roll 1d46 to randomly determine a character's background. If you prefer, you may also choose any background from the compendium directly.

results.text: Acolyte — You spent your early days in a religious monastery or cloister. Skills: Religion, Empathy, History.
Archaeologist — Crumbling ruins called to you louder than the comforts of civilization. Skills: History, Investigate.
Artisan — You practiced a particular form of building or crafting, with strong guild connections. Skills: Craft (choice), Society, Persuasion, Devices.
Artist — Your art is your greatest passion; you adventure for inspiration or to survive until the world recognizes your genius. Skills: Perform, Perception.
Bandit — You took what you needed from those who had more than enough. Skills: Stealth, Intimidate, Wilderness, Melee Combat.
Barber — Your three specialties: cutting hair, treating wounds, and listening to gossip. Skills: Medicine, Society, Persuasion, Devices.
Barkeep — You poured drinks and kept the peace where ale flowed as freely as stories. Skills: Empathy, Persuasion, Society, Perception.
Barrister — Legal study and courtroom experience instructed you in law. Skills: Society, Persuasion.
Bookkeeper — You tracked accounts and managed ledgers, developing an eye for discrepancies. Skills: Society, Investigate, Writing, Perception.
Butcher — Your apprenticeship involved sharp knives, practical anatomy, and demanding physical work. Skills: Might, Medicine, Craft: Cook, Society.
Carpenter — You shaped wood into structures that will outlast you, learning patience and precision. Skills: Craft: Woodworker, Might, Devices, Perception.
Charlatan — You traveled from place to place, peddling false fortunes and pretending to be someone you weren't. Skills: Deception, Persuasion, Perform, Thievery.
Cook — You transformed ingredients into something that warmed both body and spirit. Skills: Craft: Cook, Wilderness, Perception, Society.
Courtier — You navigated court life where every smile hides an agenda. Skills: Persuasion, Society, Empathy, Perform.
Courier — You raced against weather, bandits, and deadlines carrying messages of great importance. Skills: Athletics, Society, Perception, Wilderness.
Criminal — You lived a life of crime as an independent or as part of an underworld organization. Skills: Stealth, Thievery, Deception, Intimidate.
Driver — You hauled cargo and transported passengers, developing a reputation for getting through when others turned back. Skills: Wilderness, Athletics, Society, Perception.
Entertainer — You performed as an actor, dancer, musician, or street magician. Skills: Perform, Persuasion, Acrobatics, Perception.
Farmer — You tilled the land and tended crops with a strong back and an understanding of seasonal cycles. Skills: Wilderness, Might, Athletics, Craft: Cook.
Fisher — The water was your livelihood and your teacher; patience is a virtue you've mastered. Skills: Wilderness, Athletics, Perception, Craft: Cook.
Gambler — The thrill of the win drew you into games of chance — and you definitely know how to cheat. Skills: Deception, Empathy, Perception, Society.
Guard — You served in the guard, knowing the boredom of endless patrols punctuated by sudden terror. Skills: Melee Combat, Perception, Intimidate, Society.
Herbalist — You learned the healing properties of various herbs as a trained apothecary or folk practitioner. Skills: Medicine, Wilderness.
Hermit — You lived a life of solitude, likely rough around the edges but possessing hard-won wisdom. Skills: Wilderness, Medicine, Religion.
Hunter — You stalked and took down animals and creatures of the wild. The skills transfer remarkably well. Skills: Wilderness, Ranged Combat, Stealth, Perception.
Investigator — You made your living uncovering truths that others wanted hidden. Skills: Investigate, Perception.
Laborer — You spent years performing arduous physical labor and are trusted by working folk. Skills: Might, Athletics, Devices, Craft: Mason.
Mercenary — You fought in petty wars and brought in lawbreakers for coin. Skills: Melee Combat, Athletics, Intimidate, Wilderness.
Merchant — You bartered wares for coin, understanding that everything has a price. Skills: Persuasion, Society, Empathy, Investigate.
Miner — You wrenched precious minerals from the lightless depths of the earth. Skills: Might, Athletics, Perception, Craft: Mason.
Noble — You were born into privilege and obligation in equal measure. Skills: Society, Persuasion, History.
Nomad — You traveled far and wide, surviving on the road with few supplies and even fewer comforts. Skills: Wilderness, Athletics, Perception, Society.
Refugee — You fled your homeland due to war, disaster, or persecution and survived against the odds. Skills: Stealth, Wilderness, Empathy, Society.
Sage — You have an insatiable desire for learning and sequestered yourself from the world to pursue it. Skills: Arcane, History.
Sailor — You heard the call of the sea and answered it — merchant vessel, navy, or pirate crew. Skills: Athletics, Acrobatics, Perception, Wilderness.
Scholar — You pursued formal education at a university or academy, studying philosophy, sciences, and the arts. Skills: History, Society.
Servant — You served in a noble household, learning to be efficient, discreet, and above all, invisible. Skills: Empathy, Society, Perception, Stealth.
Shepherd — You spent long days watching over flocks, responsible for their safety in all weather. Skills: Wilderness, Perception, Athletics, Medicine.
Smith — Fire, hammer, and patience were your tools; you shaped metal with skill and care. Skills: Craft: Blacksmith, Might.
Smuggler — You moved goods past customs agents and border guards through routes authorities don't patrol. Skills: Stealth, Deception, Society, Perception.
Soldier — In your younger days, you waded into battle as part of an organized military force. Skills: Melee Combat, Athletics, Intimidate.
Squire — You served a knight or noble warrior, learning combat, chivalry, and the weight of responsibility. Skills: Melee Combat, Athletics, Society, Empathy.
Tailor — You created everything from simple work clothes to elaborate gowns, learning that clothes reveal character. Skills: Craft: Tailor, Society, Perception, Persuasion.
Teacher — You dedicated yourself to sharing knowledge, learning patience and how every student learns differently. Skills: Society, History, Persuasion, Empathy.
Urchin — You eked out a living picking pockets on city streets, where survival was the only lesson that mattered. Skills: Stealth, Thievery, Acrobatics, Society.
Ward — You were raised in a noble household, educated and cared for but never quite family. Skills: Society, History, Empathy, Perception.
